methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Commonwealth Fusion Systems

private.name: Cemvita Factory
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $120M

data.narrative:
Pano AI, a Series B climate and energy company founded by Sonia Kastner, has raised $89M to date and carries a current post-money valuation of $500M+. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of five private climate and energy companies at the same stage, with valuations ranging from $120M (Cemvita Factory) to $5.0B (Commonwealth Fusion Systems), alongside one public comparable.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B to subsequent-stage advancement, projects the $500M base to $950M. Peer-median analysis across the five same-sector, same-stage comparables yields $310M, with the peer group dispersed between $120M and $5.0B. The wide peer dispersion—driven largely by Commonwealth Fusion's $5.0B outlier position—introduces material range uncertainty in the median method.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $264M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $630M. The current $500M+ valuation sits near the lower end of this range. Key uncertainties include company-specific unit economics relative to the median peer cohort, revenue trajectory and margins not quantified in the provided data, and the structural heterogeneity within climate-tech valuations reflected in the five-fold range of peer comparables. Resolution of these factors will be material to positioning within the derived range.
